summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

If u = <4, -2> and v = <-3, 5>, what is u - v?

u - v = <4-(-3), -2-5> = <7, -7>. Vector subtraction subtracts corresponding components.

What is the magnitude of 3<-4, 3>?

3<-4, 3> = <-12, 9>. Magnitude = sqrt(144 + 81) = sqrt(225) = 15. Alternatively, |cv| = |c| * |v| = 3 * sqrt(16+9) = 3 * 5 = 15.

If |v| = 7 and v points in the direction of the unit vector <3/5, -4/5>, what is v?

v = |v| * unit vector = 7 * <3/5, -4/5> = <21/5, -28/5>. The vector has magnitude 7 in the specified direction.

What is the opposite (negative) of the vector <-3, 7>?

The opposite of v = is -v = <-a, -b>. So the opposite of <-3, 7> is <3, -7>. It has the same magnitude but points in the opposite direction.

What is scalar multiplication of a vector?

Scalar multiplication means multiplying a vector by a real number (scalar). If c > 0, the vector is scaled in the same direction; if c < 0, the direction is reversed. The magnitude becomes |c| times the original magnitude.

What is the triangle inequality for vectors?

The triangle inequality states |u + v| <= |u| + |v|. Equality holds only when u and v point in exactly the same direction. This mirrors the geometric fact that any side of a triangle is shorter than the sum of the other two sides.

What is a linear combination of vectors?

A linear combination of vectors v1, v2, ..., vn is an expression c1*v1 + c2*v2 + ... + cn*vn where c1, c2, ..., cn are scalars. Any vector in 2D can be written as a linear combination of two non-parallel vectors.

What is vector subtraction?

Vector subtraction u - v is defined as adding u to the negative of v: u - v = u + (-v). In component form, . Geometrically, u - v points from the tip of v to the tip of u when both start at the same point.

What is the commutative property of vector addition?

The commutative property states that u + v = v + u for all vectors u and v. The order of addition does not affect the result. This is shown geometrically by the parallelogram law.

What is the associative property of vector addition?

The associative property states that (u + v) + w = u + (v + w). When adding three or more vectors, the grouping (which two you add first) does not change the final result.

What is the distributive property of scalar multiplication over vector addition?

The distributive property states c(u + v) = cu + cv. Scalar multiplication distributes over vector addition, just as multiplication distributes over addition for real numbers.

What is the tip-to-tail method of vector addition?

The tip-to-tail (head-to-tail) method places vectors sequentially so each new vector's tail starts at the previous vector's tip. The resultant vector goes from the tail of the first vector to the tip of the last.

A boat travels at 15 km/h on a heading of 040° (measured clockwise from north). A current pushes the boat at 3 km/h on a heading of 130°. What is the boat's resultant speed (magnitude of the resultant velocity)?

Converting headings to standard angles: 040° heading = 50° from east, 130° heading = -40° from east. Boat: <15cos(50°), 15sin(50°)> ≈ <9.64, 11.49>. Current: <3cos(-40°), 3sin(-40°)> ≈ <2.30, -1.93>. Sum ≈ <11.94, 9.56>. |R| = sqrt(142.6 + 91.4) ≈ sqrt(234) ≈ 15.3 km/h.

An engineer needs to find the equilibrant force for two forces: F1 = <500, 0> N and F2 = <0, 300> N. The equilibrant is the negative of the resultant. What is the equilibrant?

The resultant is F1 + F2 = <500, 300> N. The equilibrant is the negative of the resultant: <-500, -300> N. This force would balance the system, putting it in equilibrium.

Three forces act on an object: F1 = <10, 0>, F2 = <-3, 8>, and F3 = <-7, -8>. What is the net force?

Net force = F1 + F2 + F3 = <10+(-3)+(-7), 0+8+(-8)> = <0, 0>. The net force is zero, meaning the object is in equilibrium.

A plane flies at a velocity of v_plane = <200, 100> km/h relative to the air. A wind blows with velocity v_wind = <-50, 30> km/h. What is the ground speed (magnitude of the resultant)?

Ground velocity = <200+(-50), 100+30> = <150, 130>. Ground speed = sqrt(150^2 + 130^2) = sqrt(22500 + 16900) = sqrt(39400) ≈ 198.5 km/h.

A construction crane lifts a steel beam. Two cables pull with forces F1 = 1000 and F2 = 1000. What is the total upward (y-component) force?

y-components: 1000 sin(70°) + 1000 sin(110°). Since sin(110°) = sin(70°), total y = 2000 sin(70°) ≈ 2000(0.9397) ≈ 1879.4 N. The horizontal components cancel since cos(110°) = -cos(70°).

A surveyor walks from base camp to three landmarks: 500 m at 30°, then 700 m at 150°, then 400 m at 270°. What is the magnitude of the total displacement from base camp?

d1 = <500cos30°, 500sin30°> = <250sqrt(3), 250>. d2 = <700cos150°, 700sin150°> = <-350sqrt(3), 350>. d3 = <400cos270°, 400sin270°> = <0, -400>. Total = <(250-350)sqrt(3), 250+350-400> = <-100sqrt(3), 200>. |R| = sqrt(30000 + 40000) = sqrt(70000) ≈ 264.6 m.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: What is scalar multiplication of a vector?

Scalar multiplication means multiplying a vector by a real number (scalar). If c > 0, the vector is scaled in the same direction; if c < 0, the direction is reversed. The magnitude becomes |c| times the original magnitude.

Example: What is the distributive property of scalar multiplication over vector addition?

The distributive property states c(u + v) = cu + cv. Scalar multiplication distributes over vector addition, just as multiplication distributes over addition for real numbers.

Example: What is the tip-to-tail method of vector addition?

The tip-to-tail (head-to-tail) method places vectors sequentially so each new vector's tail starts at the previous vector's tip. The resultant vector goes from the tail of the first vector to the tip of the last.

Example: A package hangs from two cables. Cable A has tension T_A at 50° above horizontal pulling left, and cable B has tension T_B at 35° above horizontal pulling right. If the package weighs 200 N, find T_A. (Use T_A cos(50°) = T_B cos(35°) and T_A sin(50°) + T_B sin(35°) = 200)

From T_A cos(50°) = T_B cos(35°): T_B = T_A cos(50°)/cos(35°). Substituting: T_A sin(50°) + T_A cos(50°)sin(35°)/cos(35°) = 200. T_A(sin(50°) + cos(50°)tan(35°)) = 200. T_A(0.766 + 0.643*0.700) = 200. T_A(0.766 + 0.450) = 200. T_A(1.216) = 200. T_A ≈ 164.5 N. Let me recalculate: T_A(0.766 + 0.6428 * 0.7002) = T_A(0.766 + 0.450) = T_A * 1.216 = 200, giving T_A ≈ 128.6 N after correcting tan(35°) ≈ 0.700.
